title: Should I Take an Early Morning Class in College?
author: Tklesson Team
content:
You wake up at 6:30 AM for high school. You're tired, but you do it. College will be the same, right? Wrong. An 8:00 AM class in high school is not the same as an 8:00 AM class in college. Here is the brutal reality of morning scheduling.

You're in Control of Your Sleep

In high school, your parents woke you up. You had a set bedtime (even if you ignored it). Your schedule was fixed. In college, no one wakes you up. No one tells you to go to bed. You are responsible for getting yourself to class.

The first week of college, you'll be excited. You'll wake up for 8:00 AM classes. By week six, you'll be sleeping through them. The novelty wears off. The responsibility remains.

If you are not a morning person, do not pretend you will become one in college. You won't. Be honest with yourself.

The Social Cost of Early Classes

College social life happens at night. Study groups meet in the evening. Club meetings run late. Friends want to hang out after dinner. If you have an 8:00 AM class, you'll have to leave early or show up exhausted.

Missing out on social activities affects your friendships. Showing up exhausted affects your grades. You can't have both. Choose wisely.

Some students can handle late nights and early mornings. Most cannot. Know your limits.

The Commute Reality

In high school, your commute was the same every day. In college, your classes are scattered across campus. An 8:00 AM class means leaving your dorm by 7:30 AM. That means waking up by 7:00 AM to shower, eat, and get ready. That means going to bed by 11:00 PM to get 8 hours.

If you live off campus, add commuting time. An 8:00 AM class might mean waking at 6:00 AM. That's brutal.

Check the location of your 8:00 AM class. Is it across campus from your last class the night before? Factor that in.

The Attendance Trap

Many professors don't take attendance. You can skip class. You will be tempted. Once you skip one 8:00 AM class, it's easier to skip another. Soon you've missed three weeks and you're lost.

If you don't go to class, you won't learn. If you don't learn, you won't pass. If you don't pass, you waste tuition money. Early classes require discipline you might not have.

Who Should Take 8:00 AM Classes?

Take an early class if:
• You're genuinely a morning person (you wake up early even on weekends)
• You have to (the class is only offered at 8:00 AM)
• You work better in the morning and want to get classes over with early
• You have afternoon commitments (sports practice, job) that prevent later classes

Avoid early classes if:
• You struggle to wake up now
• You value late-night socializing
• You have a long commute
• You have the option to take the same class later in the day

You can take an 8:00 AM class and survive. Many students do. But know what you're signing up for. The first week is easy. The rest of the semester is not.
